evidence.title: Detailed Description
evidence.snippet:
PRIMARY OBJECTIVES:

I. To determine the safety, biodistribution and dosimetric properties of 18F-alphavbeta6-binding peptide (BP) in normal tissues and malignancies in cancer patients and correlate concordance with alphavbeta6 expression.

OUTLINE:

Patients receive 18F-alphavbeta6-BP intravenously (IV) and then undergo positron emission tomography (PET) scans over 30 minutes each at 30, 60, 120, and 180 minutes post-injection.

After completion of study, patients are followed up for up to 6 mo

opinions.reasoning: The trial explicitly lists "18F-αvβ6-BP" as a drug. The name includes "peptide," indicating a peptide component.

The trial's name, description, and drug name all explicitly reference a peptide, and the context of the trial (PET imaging using a radiolabeled peptide) confirms that the intervention contains a peptide molecule.

According to the critical rules, if the trial description or intervention name says 'peptide', this is strong evidence for True. In this case, the intervention name includes both "peptide" and "binding-peptide", indicating that it is a peptide-based radiotracer.

The peptide is designed to bind to integrins to facilitate imaging, not to directly kill or inhibit pathogens (Mode A), recruit immune cells (Mode B), or disrupt biofilms (Mode C). Therefore, it does not meet the criteria for classification as an AMP.

The peptide is used as a targeting agent for imaging purposes, not as an antimicrobial agent. It does not contribute to pathogen defense through any of the three modes (A, B, or C) outlined in the criteria. Therefore, it is classified as "Other."

The trial's primary objectives are to determine the safety, biodistribution, and dosimetric properties of 18F-alphavbeta6-binding peptide in normal tissues and malignancies. The study involves PET scans to image patients with cancer, indicating that the peptide is used for diagnostic purposes rather than as an antimicrobial agent. Additionally, none of the published results suggest any direct antimicrobial activity or mechanism of action consistent with Modes A, B, or C.

citations.title: First-in-Human Positron Emission Tomography Study Using the 18F-αvβ6-Binding-Peptide
citations.snippet: Title: First-in-Human Positron Emission Tomography Study Using the 18F-αvβ6-Binding-Peptide

citations.title: Brief Summary
citations.snippet: This clinical trial studies the side effects of 18F-alphavbeta6-binding-peptide and how well it works in imaging patients with primary or cancer that has spread to the breast, colorectal, lung, or pancreatic. Radiotracers, such as 18F-alphavbeta6-binding-peptide, may improve the ability to locate cancer in the body.

citations.title: Detailed Description
citations.snippet:
PRIMARY OBJECTIVES:

I. To determine the safety, biodistribution and dosimetric properties of 18F-alphavbeta6-binding peptide (BP) in normal tissues and malignancies in cancer patients and correlate concordance with alphavbeta6 expression.

OUTLINE:

Patients receive 18F-alphavbeta6-BP intravenously (IV) and then undergo positron emission tomography (PET) scans over 30 minutes each at 30, 60, 120, and 180 minutes post-injection.

After completion of study, patients are followed up for up to 6 mo

citations.title: Study Status
citations.snippet: Status: ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING

citations.title: Intervention: 18F-αvβ6-BP
citations.snippet: DRUG: 18F-αvβ6-BP - Subjects will be injected once with up to 10 mCi of 18F-αvβ6-BP as a rapid intravenous bolus (within 30 seconds).

citations.title: Arm Group: 18F-αvβ6-BP
citations.snippet: Arm: 18F-αvβ6-BP - Patients receive 18F-alphavbeta6-BP IV and then undergo 4 PET scans over 30 minutes each at 30, 60, 120, and 180 minutes post-injection.

citations.title: Phase
citations.snippet: Phase: EARLY_PHASE1

descriptionModule.detailedDescription:
PRIMARY OBJECTIVES:

I. To determine the safety, biodistribution and dosimetric properties of 18F-alphavbeta6-binding peptide (BP) in normal tissues and malignancies in cancer patients and correlate concordance with alphavbeta6 expression.

OUTLINE:

Patients receive 18F-alphavbeta6-BP intravenously (IV) and then undergo positron emission tomography (PET) scans over 30 minutes each at 30, 60, 120, and 180 minutes post-injection.

After completion of study, patients are followed up for up to 6 months.

interventions.description: Subjects will be injected once with up to 10 mCi of 18F-αvβ6-BP as a rapid intravenous bolus (within 30 seconds).

primaryOutcomes.description: Assessed by measures and/or changes in a given vital sign

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Level of αvβ6-BP expression in tumors
secondaryOutcomes.description: Immunohistochemistry (IHC) staining for the cell surface receptor integrin

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Diagnosed with primary or metastatic cancer in one or more of the following locations: breast, colorectal, lung, pancreas
* Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance score of 0-1
* Will sign the Institutional Review Board (IRB)-approved consent form
* Able to remain motionless for up to 30-60 minutes per scan

Exclusion Criteria:

* Creatinine \> 2 x upper limit of normal
* Aspartate aminotransferase (AST)/alanine aminotransferase (ALT) \> 2 x upper limit of normal
* Life expectancy \< 3 months (mo)
* Women who are pregnant or breast-feeding
* Patients who cannot undergo PET/compute tomography (CT) scanning
* Lack of availability for follow-up assessments
* Participation in another clinical trial involving an investigational agent within 4 weeks of enrollment
